rationale.R1.3: This is the explicit delegation: determine whether each stated claim is grounded in the reported evidence. Three acute misalignments exist: (1) Table 1 shows StanRoute KL=0.92 vs. baseline KL=0.47 — a higher KL divergence from oracle is worse calibration, yet the paper asserts the opposite; (2) 'uniformly improves across all task families' is asserted from a single family (code generation) shown in Table 2; (3) 'preferable at any operating point on the cost-quality frontier' is asserted without reporting cost metrics at multiple operating points.
rationale.R1.2: Verifying evidence-claim links necessarily requires tracing the logical steps that connect data to conclusions. The KL argument contains an internal logical flaw — the paper conflates a higher KL score with better calibration without inversion, reversal, or sign-flip justification. The inductive leap from one task-family result to a universal claim ('all families', 'any deployment') is also a logical non-sequitur that must be flagged under this dimension.
